design_philosophy.core_loop: Plan city infrastructure → manage resources → respond to emergent problems → expand and optimize
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing fiscal responsibility against citizen demands and natural disasters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Architect and mayor of a thriving metropolis with full control over urban development
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unapologetic simulation-first design; city success is determined by infrastructure planning and budget discipline, not narrative progression. Game rewards systemic thinking over reflexive action.

difficulty.checkpointing: Save-based progression; player can load previous city states
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Extensive: players set tax rates, zoning policies, service budgets, and disaster frequency; can create sandbox scenarios or accept preset challenge scenarios

progression.upgrade_system: Infrastructure unlocks based on budget availability and city development stage; service buildings (police, fire, hospitals) become available as city grows
progression.unlock_mechanism: Revenue-gated; larger tax base enables more complex municipal services
replayability_hooks: Multiple scenario challenges with specific victory conditions
Adjustable difficulty settings and disaster frequency
Different map layouts and starting conditions

world_building_techniques: Emergent narrative through city growth and decline
Demand indicators show citizen needs and satisfaction
News ticker communicates city events and disasters

faction_indicators: Residential vs commercial vs industrial zoning preferences
Public opinion on services (reflected in approval ratings)
environmental_storytelling: Visual density of buildings indicates prosperity
Empty zones indicate lack of desirability or access
